skipped_tests.name: test_t4_nve_drift
skipped_tests.reason: D-M6-8 drift gate is MixedFastBuild-only. MixedFastSnapOnlyBuild keeps EAM at FP64 (no FP32 EAM kernel linked), so the MixedFast FP32-EAM drift envelope does not apply. Correct behaviour — EAM residual here is the byte-exact FP64 reduction-order ~1e-14, well below any drift budget.

fp64_reference_comparison.interpretation: FP32 SNAP bispectrum noise dominates 10-step drift; ratio matches expectation (~70-100x) for k_max=55 accumulations.

t4_nial_alloy_differential.note: EAM runs FP64 under this flavor — residual identical to Fp64Reference.

t6_snap_differential.note: D-M8-7 byte-exact vs LAMMPS on 250-atom fixture. Flavor-orthogonal (CPU FP64 oracle path).

snap_gpu_bit_exact.note: D-M6-7 extension to SNAP GPU (landed T8.7). Flavor-orthogonal (Reference flavor compile-time).
snap_gpu_bit_exact.build_note: Self-tests when built under Fp64Reference; on non-Reference flavors it exercises the GPU bit-exact invariant the same way.

eam_mixed_fast_within_threshold.note: EAM stays FP64 in MixedFastSnapOnly — measured residual ~1e-14, nowhere near 1e-5 budget.

test_multirank_td_smoke_2rank.note: D-M7-10 byte-exact chain (M3..M7 thermo equivalence) preserved under this flavor.

findings.per_flavor_gates: ALL GREEN — every gate authoritatively registered for MixedFastSnapOnlyBuild passes. §D.17 step 5 per-flavor contract satisfied.

pre_existing_shared_bug.summary: 1000-step NVE on the T6 1024-atom fixture reproduces a runaway temperature culminating in gpu::SnapGpu::D2H cudaErrorIllegalAddress crash on BOTH Fp64ReferenceBuild and MixedFastSnapOnlyBuild, with the trajectories agreeing to ~1e-7 rel step-by-step through step 200. This is a physics + code issue not introduced by T8.8 (MixedFastSnapOnly flavor add).

observed_temperatures.step_100: 1284 K (+328% — ΔPE≈130 eV released to KE)
observed_temperatures.crash_point: between step 220 and step 1000 — cudaErrorIllegalAddress on SNAP D2H force copy

pre_existing_shared_bug.diagnosis: Initial BCC lattice at a0=3.1803 Å (upstream Wood & Thompson 2017 parameter) is ~0.13 eV/atom above the equilibrium minimum for the W_2940_2017_2 SNAP fit. NVE conservation converts that PE budget into KE over ~100 steps, heating the crystal past melting by step 200. At high T the neighbour count per atom exceeds the preallocated SNAP GPU buffers → illegal memory access.
pre_existing_shared_bug.flavor_regression_test: RESULT: NOT A MixedFastSnapOnlyBuild REGRESSION. Both flavors trip the same trap at the same step. Fp64Reference values at step 200: T=51055.055579 K, E_tot=-18215.376704 eV. MixedFastSnapOnly at step 200: T=51055.062586 K, E_tot=-18215.376767 eV. Relative agreement ~1e-7, within expected FP32 bispectrum noise.
recommended_followups: Fix the fixture: either lattice-relax the 1024-atom setup (LAMMPS minimize + write_data) before committing, or add explicit initial-relaxation steps in the integration test
Add max-neighbours guard on SNAP GPU path so high-T configurations fail gracefully with a bounded error message rather than cudaErrorIllegalAddress
Add a 100-step variant of m8_smoke_t6 to the slow-tier that would have caught this before T8.10 landed
Investigate upstream Wood & Thompson 2017 log file to see if they also observe this heating (they only run 100 steps — T ends near 1300 K consistent with our observation)

verdict.spec_d17_step5_mandate: SATISFIED — equal-or-tighter-than-MixedFast promise verified for every registered threshold
verdict.shared_regression_risk: NONE — MixedFastSnapOnly behaves identically to Fp64Reference on the same fixture
verdict.shared_preexisting_bug_severity: HIGH — blocks T8.13 v1-alpha tag until separately triaged
verdict.blocks_m8_closure_reason: Shared 1000-step crash is a new finding that was not surfaced before T8.10; safety gate at §D.15 red-flag protocol applies. Must resolve before tagging v1.0.0-alpha1.

followups.new_task_subject: T6 1024-atom fixture 1000-step stability — shared Fp64Reference + MixedFastSnapOnly temperature runaway + SNAP GPU illegal-address crash
